Q2.
Read the scenario below and answer the questions that follow related to routine graphs used in the workplace.

 

Scenario

You are currently working as an administrative assistant in a clothing store. While working, you notice a leak in one of the dressing rooms. You report this to your administrator, who then asks you to write down what you saw on the risk register, a table used to compile all the risks found within your workplace. Your administrator tells you that it’s important to record this on as soon as possible so that management can resolve the risks immediately as they found.

 

Question at position 3
3
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 3 
b) Briefly explain how the register is used in the workplace.

This essay has a maximum word count of 50.
b) Briefly explain how the register is used in the workplace.







p




50 max (word limit)

The register is used in the workplace to document and track all the risks and hazards identified within the organization. It serves as a central repository for recording and monitoring risks, allowing management to prioritize and allocate resources to address and resolve them. The register helps ensure that risks are not overlooked and are promptly addressed to maintain a safe and secure working environment.

Briefly explain why you need to record the risk into the table as soon as possible

This essay has a maximum word count of 50.
c) Briefly explain why you need to record the risk into the table as soon as possible

Recording the risk into the table as soon as possible is necessary to ensure timely management and resolution of the identified risk. By documenting it promptly, management can take immediate action to address the risk and prevent any potential accidents or damages. This helps maintain a safe and secure workplace environment for employees and customers.
Briefly explain why the graph was used in this scenario.

In this scenario, a graph was not specifically mentioned or indicated as being used. The administrator asks the employee to write down what they saw on the risk register, which is a table used to compile all the risks in the workplace. Therefore, a graph is not necessary for this particular scenario.
Briefly explain why the humidity information is presented on the graph.

In the given scenario, there is no mention of humidity information being presented on a graph. Therefore, it is not appropriate to explain the purpose or significance of any humidity information presented on a graph in this context.

Read the scenario below and answer the questions that follow related to routine graphs used in the workplace.

 

Scenario 

Robert is a farmworker. His supervisor asks him to check the humidity to see if they can continue with their schedule to plant crops for the day. Robert checks a humidity graph, which displays the level of humidity to expect for the day. He sees the forecast is relatively low humidity, so they can proceed to plant crops as planned. 

Question at position 7
7
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 7 
c) Briefly explain why the humidity information is presented on the graph.

This essay has a maximum word count of 50.
c) Briefly explain why the humidity information is presented on the graph.

The humidity information is presented on the graph to provide a visual representation of the expected humidity levels throughout the day. This helps Robert and his supervisor make informed decisions regarding their schedule for planting crops. By having the data displayed on a graph, they can easily determine if the humidity is at an appropriate level for planting and adjust their plans accordingly. This visual representation allows for quick and efficient decision-making in agriculture, where humidity can have a significant impact on crop growth and health.

Q4.
Read the scenario below and answer the questions that follow about routine charts used in the workplace. 

 

Scenario

Edna is a sales assistant for a perfume line. She is tasked to give an update on their branch’s sales to their supervisor every month. She presents the information on a sales line chart, which they created to track how the sales changes every month. In her update, she uses the sales chart to show her supervisor if sales went up or down throughout those three months. 

Question at position 9
9
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 9 
b) Briefly explain how the chart was used in th

workplace.

The chart was used in the workplace to track and visualize the changes in sales every month. By presenting the sales data on a line chart, Edna was able to clearly demonstrate the trends and fluctuations in sales over the course of three months. This allowed her to easily communicate to her supervisor whether sales had increased or decreased during that period. The chart served as a visual tool to help analyze the sales performance and make data-driven decisions for the perfume line's branch.

Q4.
Read the scenario below and answer the questions that follow about routine charts used in the workplace. 

 

Scenario

Edna is a sales assistant for a perfume line. She is tasked to give an update on their branch’s sales to their supervisor every month. She presents the information on a sales line chart, which they created to track how the sales changes every month. In her update, she uses the sales chart to show her supervisor if sales went up or down throughout those three months. 

Question at position 10
10
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 10 
c) Briefly explain why the sales are presented on the chart.

The sales are presented on the chart to visually represent and track the changes in sales over the course of the three months. By using a line chart, Edna is able to clearly show the fluctuations in sales performance throughout the period. This visual representation allows for easy comparison and identification of trends, such as whether sales have increased or decreased. By presenting the sales data on a chart, it becomes easier for Edna and her supervisor to analyze the sales performance and make informed decisions to improve sales and business outcomes.

Q5.
Read the scenario below and answer the questions that follow about routine charts used in the workplace. 

 

Scenario 

John is an administrative assistant. For their monthly office update, he was tasked by their office manager to gather the office’s power consumption information from the last three months. He was then tasked to present it on a power consumption chart to show the change in power consumption during that period. The chart was to be used to show how changing the office lights to LED lights the previous month helped the office conserve electricity.

Question at position 12
12
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 12 
b) Briefly explain how the chart was used in this scenario.

The chart was used in this scenario to visually represent and demonstrate the change in power consumption over the last three months. John gathered the power consumption information and presented it on a power consumption chart. The purpose of using the chart was to highlight the impact of switching the office lights to LED lights in the previous month on electricity conservation. By presenting this data on a chart, it becomes easier to analyze and compare the power consumption patterns before and after the switch to LED lights. This visual representation helps the office manager and other stakeholders understand the effectiveness of the change and make informed decisions regarding energy conservation measures in the future.

Q5.
Read the scenario below and answer the questions that follow about routine charts used in the workplace. 

 

Scenario 

John is an administrative assistant. For their monthly office update, he was tasked by their office manager to gather the office’s power consumption information from the last three months. He was then tasked to present it on a power consumption chart to show the change in power consumption during that period. The chart was to be used to show how changing the office lights to LED lights the previous month helped the office conserve electricity.

Question at position 13
13
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 13 
c) Briefly explain why the power consumption information is presented on a chart.

The power consumption information is presented on a chart to visually display and communicate the changes in power consumption over the last three months. By using a chart, John can present the data in a clear and easily understandable format. This enables the office manager and other stakeholders to visually analyze the trends and patterns in power consumption. Additionally, the chart allows for a quick and effective comparison of power consumption before and after the switch to LED lights. By presenting the information on a chart, it becomes easier to identify the impact of the office's efforts to conserve electricity and facilitates decision-making regarding energy-saving measures in the future.

List two methods to perform calculations with statistical data.

 

Statistical data may include, but is not limited to, the following:

Total number in a row
Difference of two data pieces 
This essay has a maximum word count of 100.
List two methods to perform calculations with statistical data.

 

Statistical data may include, but is not limited to, the following:

Total number in a row
Difference of two data pieces

1. The first method to perform calculations with statistical data is by using the sum or total. This involves adding up all the values in a row or column to obtain the total number. For example, if you have a dataset with the number of sales made by different sales representatives in a month, you can calculate the total number of sales by adding up all the values in the dataset.

2. The second method is to calculate the difference between two data pieces. This can be done by subtracting the value of one data piece from another. For instance, if you have a dataset with the monthly revenue of a company for two consecutive years, you can calculate the difference in revenue between the two years by subtracting the revenue of the first year from the revenue of the second year.

By using these methods, one can perform calculations on statistical data to extract meaningful insights and analyze trends or patterns.

Briefly explain the method for calculating each type of statistical information listed below.

 



 

Question at position 21
21
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 21 
a) In your own words, what does Average mean?

Average, also known as the arithmetic mean, is a statistical measure that represents the central tendency of a set of data. It is calculated by summing up all the values in the dataset and dividing the sum by the total number of values. 

To calculate the average, you add up all the values in the dataset and then divide the sum by the number of values. For example, if you have a dataset with the daily temperatures for a week (e.g., 22°C, 24°C, 23°C, 25°C, 20°C, 21°C, 19°C), you would add up all the values (22 + 24 + 23 + 25 + 20 + 21 + 19 = 154) and divide it by the number of values in the dataset (7). The average temperature would then be 154 / 7 = 22°C. 

The average provides a representative value that summarizes the dataset and gives an indication of the typical or central value of the data. It helps in understanding the overall trend or central tendency of the dataset, making it a widely used and important measure in statistical analysis.

Read the scenario below. 

 

After an advertising agency sent out a survey and found that around 90% of the respondents use their soap, they put out an advertisement for bath soap saying, ‘9 out of 10 Australians use our product daily.’ A month after the advertisement was released, even more people purchased the product, resulting in an increase in sales.

 

Identify the method to use data and information to persuade used in each scenario. 

This essay has a maximum word count of 50.
Read the scenario below. 

 

After an advertising agency sent out a survey and found that around 90% of the respondents use their soap, they put out an advertisement for bath soap saying, ‘9 out of 10 Australians use our product daily.’ A month after the advertisement was released, even more people purchased the product, resulting in an increase in sales.

 

Identify the method to use data and information to persuade used in each scenario.

In the given scenario, the method used to persuade through data and information is the appeal to popularity or the bandwagon effect. By stating that "9 out of 10 Australians use our product daily," the advertising agency is using the statistic from their survey to imply that their soap is widely popular among the majority of Australians. This technique aims to convince potential customers to join the perceived majority and use their product. The increase in sales a month after the advertisement suggests that the advertising strategy was successful in persuading more people to purchase their soap based on the perception of its popularity.

Read the scenario below. 

 

A welfare organisation was tasked to help victims of flood in a poor community. They assessed the situation and found that 80% of the families lost all of their possessions to the flood. They included this statistic in an email to their supporters to encourage them to give what they can to the community. This resulted in many people donating, completing the amount they need to help the community.

 

Identify the method to use data and information to persuade used in each scenario. 

This essay has a maximum word count of 50.
Read the scenario below. 

 

A welfare organisation was tasked to help victims of flood in a poor community. They assessed the situation and found that 80% of the families lost all of their possessions to the flood. They included this statistic in an email to their supporters to encourage them to give what they can to the community. This resulted in many people donating, completing the amount they need to help the community.

 

Identify the method to use data and information to persuade used in each scenario.

In the given scenario, the method used to persuade through data and information is appeal to sympathy or empathy. By including the statistic that "80% of the families lost all of their possessions to the flood" in the email to their supporters, the welfare organization is using this data to evoke emotions of sympathy and empathy. They aim to create a sense of urgency and encourage their supporters to donate generously, as they emphasize the devastating impact the flood has had on the community. This technique taps into the supporters' compassion and empathy for the victims, motivating them to contribute to the cause and help the community in need. The result of many people donating showcases the effectiveness of this persuasive approach.

Read the scenario below. 

 

ALL Membership Shopping wanted to find out the products their members looked for most often in their stores. They have 5000 members, and they sent out surveys to 100 members. However, the ideal sample size is 10% of all their members, which is 500 members. 

 

Identify the method used to identify misleading data 

This essay has a maximum word count of 50.
Read the scenario below. 

 

ALL Membership Shopping wanted to find out the products their members looked for most often in their stores. They have 5000 members, and they sent out surveys to 100 members. However, the ideal sample size is 10% of all their members, which is 500 members. 

 

Identify the method used to identify misleading data

In the given scenario, the method used to identify misleading data is the use of an insufficient sample size. By sending out surveys to only 100 members out of the total 5000 members, ALL Membership Shopping is using a sample size that is much smaller than the recommended ideal size of 10% (500 members). This small sample size can lead to biased or unreliable results because it may not adequately represent the preferences and behaviors of the entire membership base. Therefore, the method used in this scenario highlights the potential for misleading data due to an insufficient sample size.

Read the scenario below. 

 

Jen is an administrative assistant of an electrical company. The company conducted a study on how households consume electricity daily. They presented the results on a pie graph. However, when Jen added up the sectors of the pie graph to check if they equal 100%, she found that they only added up to 80%.

 

Identify the method used to identify misleading data 

This essay has a maximum word count of 50.
Read the scenario below. 

 

Jen is an administrative assistant of an electrical company. The company conducted a study on how households consume electricity daily. They presented the results on a pie graph. However, when Jen added up the sectors of the pie graph to check if they equal 100%, she found that they only added up to 80%.

 

Identify the method used to identify misleading data

In the given scenario, the method used to identify misleading data is verifying the accuracy of calculations. By adding up the sectors of the pie graph and finding that they only added up to 80% instead of the expected 100%, Jen has identified a discrepancy in the data presentation. This indicates a potential error in the calculation or representation of the data, which may lead to a misleading interpretation of the results. Verifying the accuracy of calculations is an essential method to identify misleading data and ensure the integrity and reliability of the information presented.
